name: peasant-hovel
summary: A peasant's one-room hovel — dirt floor, central fire, a straw pallet in the corner, animals possibly sharing the space. Subsistence.

primary_palette.note: classic hovel — central firepit, no chimney, smoke escapes through roof
slightly better-off peasant — wall hearth, two sleeping pallets
peasant-and-beast — a goat or pig shares the hovel behind a low partition

story_prompts: occupied — a family of three huddled around the firepit, the baby crying
the lord's men have come for the tithe — the hovel is being turned out
the peasant is dead of fever — the hovel to be burned at dawn
refugees squat here — not the usual occupants, strangers uneasy in the space
the hovel hides something — a cache beneath the hearthstone, a runaway in the hay
anti_patterns: NO furniture beyond the barest minimum — no chairs, no wardrobes, no shelves
no windows implied — hovels are dark. Light is fire.
the floor is earth or straw, not planks
clutter is RAGS and TOOLS, not possessions — peasants own almost nothing
